summary: Built-in example development workflow profiles for agent-os.
override_policy: A direct user instruction for the current repo or session overrides the active profile unless it would discard work, publish externally, or perform a destructive operation.

axes.dock: Durable agent role, authority boundary, handoff contract, stop conditions, and lifecycle hooks.
axes.entry_path: Active capability layer for the current task, such as Agent harness, AOS developer, testing, visual diagnostics, or user-input diagnostics.
axes.workflow_profile: Development integration policy: branch, commit, review, PR, merge authority, and release posture.

profiles.name: Agentic Relay
intended_for: single developer with an occasional remote Foreman-compatible relay
local GDI implementation, whether native subagent or explicitly assigned terminal relay, coordinating with a GitHub-only review authority
workflows where a pushed branch is the durable handoff artifact between local and remote sessions
profiles.main_role: stable integration target; GDI never lands implementation work without a designated relay authority
profiles.review_default: designated relay authority reads the remote branch and merges or flags; the authority can be a remote relay, human, or local Foreman acting as relay
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on a gdi/<slug> branch; push branch to origin at completion
profiles.branch_strategy: one branch per work card; named gdi/<work-card-slug>; branch is the remote-visible relay artifact and is deleted after merge
branch_optional_for: docs-only or config-only changes that carry no implementation risk
profiles.relay_merge_authority: designated Foreman-compatible relay authority with GitHub repository access; often remote and without local checkout
agent_guidance: The assigned GDI executor creates a branch named gdi/<work-card-slug> from main before starting any implementation work.
The assigned GDI executor commits verified work to that branch and pushes it to origin at completion when the profile and dispatch explicitly authorize publication.
GDI reports the branch name, HEAD SHA, verification, and local-only state in the completion report. GDI does not merge to main.
The relay authority reads the remote branch, evaluates the work, and merges to main or requests changes.
When the relay authority is remote, it performs the Foreman review/merge function through GitHub-visible evidence and may request local probes for facts it cannot observe.
A local Foreman session may act as the relay authority when the remote relay is absent, interrupted, or explicitly handed off.
Work card Git sections should instruct GDI to push the branch and report branch name + SHA, not to push main.

profiles.name: Local Relay
intended_for: single developer with tight Foreman-human-dock-team loops
local GDI implementation subagent reviewed by local Foreman or the human
workflows that need branches and stashes without linked git worktrees or automatic pushes
profiles.default_work_surface: single checkout at /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os on the current local branch
profiles.main_role: stable integration target; Foreman decides when local branch work is merged or published
profiles.review_default: local Foreman or human reviews local commits and verification before merge or push; Foreman may offer an explicitly approved short-lived PR when outsider review would be useful
profiles.commit_strategy: small scoped local commits on the current branch or one local gdi/<slug> branch; no automatic push
profiles.branch_strategy: use the single repo checkout; create or switch local branches only when Foreman or the work card names them; never create linked git worktrees
branch_required_for: dirty baseline that must be preserved
work that needs a local review checkpoint before merge
branch_optional_for: Foreman-human direct loop on the current branch
outsider review checkpoint after local acceptance
profiles.relay_merge_authority: local Foreman or the human using local branch state; GitHub publication is explicit, not automatic
profiles.branch_naming_convention: gdi/<work-card-slug> or foreman/<slug> when Foreman owns the slice
agent_guidance: Work only in /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os; do not create linked git worktrees or put repo-mode binaries outside the main checkout.
Use branches, scoped commits, and named stashes to preserve unrelated work before switching context.
GDI may commit locally only when the work card says to produce a checkpoint; GDI does not push, open PRs, merge, or clean branches unless explicitly assigned.
Foreman reviews local branch state, verification, and stashes before deciding merge, push, PR, or cleanup.

After accepting a slice, Foreman should execute the next reversible local step instead of stopping at acceptance: run missing evidence, commit a scoped checkpoint, update the ledger, or route the next bounded subagent task.

When the next step is external publication, merge, branch cleanup, credentials, permissions, destructive cleanup, or product judgment, Foreman must stop with an actionable gate: blocked action, recommended default, exact human approval phrase, post-approval command path, and safe alternative.

At practical review points, Foreman may suggest publishing a small focused PR for a non-local reviewer, but only as an explicit human-approved review option; it is not automatic publication and should keep scope narrow.
The repo-mode ./aos binary is stable infrastructure at /Users/Michael/Code/agent-os/aos and should not be rebuilt except for explicitly accepted native changes.

profiles.name: Hybrid Trunk
intended_for: agent relay review without mandatory pull requests
profiles.review_default: local tests, agent relay review, or human review when risk warrants it
profiles.commit_strategy: small frequent scoped commits directly on main
profiles.branch_strategy: use short-lived branches sparingly as a safety valve
branch_required_for: external collaboration requiring a pull request
agent_guidance: Prefer staying on main when the checkout is clean and the task is small.
Keep main releasable by running focused verification before checkpointing.
Use branches only when they reduce real risk or preserve unrelated work.

profiles.name: GitHub Flow
intended_for: projects using lightweight pull request review
profiles.review_default: lightweight pull request review before merge
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on a short-lived branch
profiles.branch_strategy: create one branch per feature, bugfix, refactor, or docs change
branch_optional_for: tiny emergency fix when explicitly approved
agent_guidance: Start from main, create a short-lived branch, and keep it close to main.
Use a pull request for review and merge back into main when ready.
Avoid long-running divergence.

profiles.name: GitFlow
intended_for: projects requiring separated development, release, and production branches
profiles.review_default: review before merging into develop, release, hotfix, or main branches
profiles.commit_strategy: scoped commits on feature, release, or hotfix branches
profiles.branch_strategy: use named develop, feature/*, release/*, and hotfix/* branches
agent_guidance: Do not work directly on main for normal development.
Branch day-to-day work from develop unless the task is a production hotfix.
Use release branches to stabilize releases before merging to main.
